channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Israel's Remarkable Journey: From Exile to Nationhood
item.description: This week marks the 78th anniversary of Israel's independence, highlighting the extraordinary journey of the Jewish people who overcame centuries of persecution and hardship. Founded in 1948 after a l...

item.title: Tornado Causes Significant Damage in Oklahoma, Disrupting Roads and Homes
item.description: A powerful tornado swept through Oklahoma on April 24, 2026, causing widespread damage to homes and infrastructure. The tornado left a trail of destruction, with multiple homes severely damaged and se...

item.title: TNT Tropang 5G overcomes Macau Black Knights in PBA, bounces back to winning form
item.description: In a thrilling match at the PBA Commissioner’s Cup, TNT Tropang 5G defeated the Macau Black Knights, reclaiming their winning streak after a tough loss. TNT had been on a challenging run but showed re...

item.title: Jalil Chooses to Leave to Ensure Community Safety in 'Blood vs Duty' Episode 15
item.description: In the fifteenth episode of 'Blood vs Duty,' the storyline intensifies as Jalil makes the difficult decision to distance himself from the community he cares deeply about. The episode focuses on the co...

item.title: EBB Air Embraer Aircraft Overruns Mandera Runway, No Injuries Reported
item.description: On Friday, an EBB Air-operated Embraer aircraft registered as 5Y-EBB had a runway excursion at Mandera Airstrip while landing from Nairobi. The plane failed to stop within the runway limits and ended ...

item.title: Glasgow woman sentenced to 16 months for carrying cannabis through Edinburgh Airport
item.description: A 23-year-old woman from Glasgow, Ellie Crampsie, has been sentenced to 16 months in prison after admitting to smuggling a significant quantity of cannabis into the United Kingdom. The offence took pl...

item.title: Exploring a Remote Mountain Kingdom Known for Its Cultural Heritage and Dramatic Landscapes
item.description: This article highlights a remote and historically rich mountain kingdom that has captured global attention for its dramatic scenery, preserved traditions, and almost mythical atmosphere. Nestled among...

item.title: Free trading offers can encourage risky behaviour, so investors should proceed cautiously
item.description: This opinion piece examines the rise of commission-free trades offered by discount brokerages such as TD Direct Investing, which recently doubled its annual free trades to 100. While these offers appe...

item.title: Generation Z drives resurgence in cinema attendance
item.description: Despite fears that younger audiences would abandon traditional cinema for digital streaming, Generation Z is now leading a revival of moviegoing. Recent surveys indicate that 87% of Gen Z have visited...

item.title: Lauren Dyogi reacts to global media attention on BINI and comments on their Coachella stage

item.title: 7 Nigerian Trailblazers Who Set Historic Milestones
item.description: Nigeria has been home to several remarkable individuals who broke barriers and achieved major milestones, both on a national and global scale. This article highlights 7 Nigerians who were the first to...

item.title: Supreme Court Registry to Pause Physical Services During Staff Retreat, Online Filing to Continue
item.description: The Judiciary in Kenya has announced a temporary suspension of physical services at the Supreme Court registry due to a planned three-day annual staff retreat scheduled from April 27 to April 29, 2026...

item.title: Teacher Couple Arrested in Ruiru Over Alleged Sh2.4 Million Land Fraud Scheme
item.description: Detectives from the Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) have arrested a couple accused of defrauding a land buyer in a Sh2.4 million scheme that was exposed during a title transfer process at...

item.title: Raghav Chadha switches from AAP to BJP, leveraging anti-defection law
item.description: Raghav Chadha, a prominent politician previously with AAP, has officially joined the BJP, causing considerable political chatter across Delhi and the nation. This move is significant not only due to t...

item.title: Chernobyl's 40th Anniversary Marked by Ongoing Threats Amid War
item.description: As Ukraine approaches the 40th anniversary of the Chernobyl disaster, the power plant continues to face significant challenges, made worse by the ongoing war. A recent drone strike targeted the protec...

item.title: Sindh Government Provides Relief to Gul Plaza Fire Victims with Rs7 Billion Package
item.description: The Sindh government, under the leadership of Chief Minister Murad Ali Shah, has approved a substantial Rs7 billion relief package for the victims of the devastating fire that engulfed Gul Plaza earli...

item.title: BagelFest West Showcases a New Era of Bagel Innovation and Tradition
item.description: At this year’s BagelFest West in Los Angeles, the humble bagel has undergone a revival, blending innovation with traditional Jewish techniques. The event, which took place on April 12, drew a crowd of...

item.title: Women in Plateau protest killings and question military effectiveness after repeated attacks
item.description: Women for Plateau State, especially from Barkin Ladi area, don come out protest serious over continuous killings and attacks wey no gree stop despite military presence. The protest show deep anger and...

item.title: BII Plans £9 Billion Investment to Boost African Economies Over Five Years
item.description: British International Investment (BII) don announce new five-year plan wey go mobilise £9 billion into Africa. Under this plan, dem go directly invest almost £5 billion while dem go try gather the res...

item.title: Athina Onassis makes a rare public appearance at Barcelona Bridal Week after years out of the spotlight
item.description: Athina Onassis, the only surviving granddaughter of Greek shipping magnate Aristotle Onassis, has made an uncommon public appearance at Barcelona Bridal Week, signalling a possible gradual return to p...
